answer:
The agreement described in the text is most similar to the **D. Kellogg-Briand Pact (1928)**.

This pact, also known as the Pact of Paris, was a multilateral treaty aimed at outlawing war as an instrument of national policy, much like the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact's focus on non-aggression and avoiding conflict. Both agreements emphasize mutual non-interference and peaceful dispute resolution, though the Molotov-Ribbentrop Pact was a bilateral non-aggression treaty rather than a general anti-war pact.
